excerpt: Every Alpha Futures rule explained in plain language — drawdown limits, profit targets, trading restrictions, and payout terms.
content:
Alpha Futures Rules Overview

Alpha Futures is a UK-based proprietary trading firm founded in 2024, headquartered in Harpenden, United Kingdom. The firm specializes in futures trading and operates a traditional evaluation model where traders start on a demo account, must hit profit targets, then graduate to a funded account with real capital.

The firm offers three distinct product lines:

  • Standard Plan — Entry-level evaluation with EOD trailing drawdown and a phased profit split (80% initially, 90% after 5 successful payouts)
  • Advanced Plan — Faster scaling with 90% profit split from day one; no consistency requirements on the qualified phase
  • Zero Plan — Smallest account sizes ($25K–$50K) designed for traders testing the platform with minimal commitment

All accounts use an end-of-day (EOD) trailing drawdown model, meaning the maximum drawdown resets daily and cannot be triggered by intraday wicks—only by closing prices. This is a significant advantage for scalp and swing traders who move in and out of positions throughout the day.

Alpha Futures distinguishes itself through a 90% profit split on the Advanced plan from day one, rapid payouts (48 business hours), and multiple international payout methods (ACH, Wire, SWIFT, Wise, Rise). The firm maintains a 4.9/5 Trustpilot rating across 2,500+ reviews, indicating high trader satisfaction.

Account Types and Pricing

Alpha Futures offers six distinct account tiers across three plan types. Pricing is monthly and locked at purchase—account upgrades or plan changes require starting a new evaluation.

Plan Type Account Size Monthly Cost Profit Split (Qualified) Best For
Zero $25K $25,000 $79/mo 90% Minimal-commitment testing
Zero $50K $50,000 $119/mo 90% Risk-averse entry point
Standard $50K $50,000 $79/mo 80% → 90% (after 5 payouts) Cost-conscious evaluation
Standard $100K $100,000 $159/mo 80% → 90% (after 5 payouts) Higher account size seekers
Advanced $50K $50,000 $139/mo 90% (from day one) Fast-track funded status
Advanced $100K $100,000 $279/mo 90% (from day one) Serious traders targeting scale

No free tier is available. All accounts require a monthly subscription fee, even during the evaluation phase. The Zero and Standard plans are the most affordable entry points, while the Advanced plans accelerate the path to profitability through the immediate 90% split.

Platform Choice Lock: Once you purchase an account, your trading platform (MT4, MT5, cTrader, or similar) is locked. You cannot switch platforms mid-account; changing platforms requires a new evaluation purchase. Plan accordingly before signing up.

Profit Targets

Alpha Futures enforces a phased profit-target structure during the evaluation period, though specific targets are not exhaustively detailed in public documentation. Based on industry standards and the firm's description, the evaluation model likely follows this framework:

  • Phase 1 (Evaluation): Traders must demonstrate consistency by hitting an initial profit target (typically 5-10% of account balance, though exact figures vary by plan). This phase tests trading discipline and risk management.
  • Phase 2 (Qualified): Once the initial target is hit and verified, traders graduate to a "qualified" or "funded" account where the profit split applies and payouts become available.

Profit Reset Policy: Most prop firms reset profit targets monthly; Alpha Futures' specific reset schedule is not detailed in available documentation. Traders should clarify with support whether targets are monthly, rolling, or tied to payout cycles.

Consistency Rules (Standard vs Advanced): The Standard plan requires consistency metrics before the profit split increases to 90%; the Advanced plan waives these restrictions from day one, meaning there is no consistency rule on the qualified phase for Advanced traders. This is a major advantage for traders who prefer immediate 90% payouts.

Drawdown Rules

Maximum Drawdown Type: Alpha Futures uses an end-of-day (EOD) trailing drawdown model. This is critical: the maximum drawdown limit resets every day at market close and is calculated only on closing prices. Intraday wicks, spike reversals, and limit-up/limit-down moves cannot trigger a drawdown breach if your position closes above the threshold.

Daily Drawdown Limit: Not explicitly stated in public documentation. Industry standards for similar firms typically range from 2–5% of daily account balance. Traders should confirm the exact daily limit with Alpha Futures support before signing up, as this impacts scalping and intraday trading strategy.

Maximum Drawdown (Trailing): The maximum drawdown limit is "trailing," meaning it anchors to the highest closing equity and cannot fall below a specified percentage (commonly 10% for Standard/Zero, 12% for Advanced, though these are estimates pending official clarification). The trailing design means you earn your way out: if you close the day at equity highs, your drawdown ceiling rises; if you drawdown, the ceiling becomes active for the next trading day.

Drawdown Reset: The EOD trailing model resets at the daily close. A drawdown breach can only occur on a closing price; opening gaps and intraday volatility are not monitored.

Impact on Trading Style: EOD trailing drawdown favors day traders and intraday scalpers, as it prevents forced liquidations from spike moves or stop-hunts. Swing traders and overnight position holders benefit less, as the daily reset provides continuous monitoring of multi-day drawdowns.

Trading Restrictions

Prohibited Strategies:

  • Expert Advisors (EAs), Bots, and Algorithmic Trading: Strictly prohibited across all three plan types. High-frequency trading (HFT) is banned. This is not a platform for automated traders or algo developers.
  • Copy Trading: Not explicitly addressed in available documentation; clarify with support if you plan to mirror trades or use copy-trading plugins.

News and Economic Event Trading:

  • Standard Plan: 2-minute news buffer enforced. You cannot enter a trade for 2 minutes before or after major economic announcements (Fed decisions, NFP, CPI, etc.). This is a soft restriction common across prop firms to reduce volatility risk.
  • Advanced Plan: News buffer restrictions are less stringent or absent; confirm with support.
  • Zero Plan: 2-minute news buffer enforced, similar to Standard.

Position Holding Rules:

  • Weekend Holding: Not explicitly prohibited. Most futures traders can hold positions overnight and over weekends, depending on the underlying market (e.g., ES/NQ trade 23 hours/day with a 1-hour close). Confirm weekend holding rules for your selected instruments.
  • Lot/Contract Size Limits: Not detailed in public documentation. Standard contracts are typically allowed; micro contracts and mini contracts may have restrictions. Check with support for exact lot limits on your account size.

Minimum and Maximum Trade Durations: Not specified. Scalping (sub-minute trades) appears to be allowed given the EOD trailing drawdown design; longer-term swing trades are unrestricted in available documentation.

Restricted Instruments: Alpha Futures specializes in futures. Forex, stocks, and crypto trading are not mentioned; the platform integrates with futures brokers. Confirm exact tradeable instruments with support.

Profit Split and Payouts

Profit Split by Plan:

  • Standard Plan: 80% to trader (on evaluation/first phase), 90% after 5 successful payouts
  • Advanced Plan: 90% from day one, no ramp-up required
  • Zero Plan: 90% profit split (equivalent to Advanced in payout structure)

The Advanced and Zero plans offer a significant advantage: immediate 90% payouts from the qualified phase without a consistency or payout ramp. This means qualified traders keep $900 profit per $1,000 earned from day one.

Payout Frequency: Alpha Futures processes payouts weekly after five winning days. This is more frequent than many competitors, which typically require monthly payouts or longer consistency windows. Weekly payouts allow traders to cash out profits more rapidly and manage risk by reducing the floating balance in the account.

Minimum Withdrawal Amount: Not specified in public documentation. Clarify with support whether there is a $100, $250, or $500 minimum withdrawal threshold.

Processing Time: Payouts are processed in 48 business hours. This is industry-standard and applies across all payout methods.

Payout Methods: Alpha Futures offers multiple international rails, a competitive advantage for international traders:

  • ACH (US): Direct bank transfer for US-based traders
  • Wire Transfer (SWIFT): International SWIFT wire for non-US traders
  • Wise (TransferWise): Multi-currency transfer service with favorable exchange rates
  • Rise: Fintech payout rail (rising in popularity among prop firms)

The multiple payout options reduce friction for international traders and minimize currency conversion costs if using Wise or Rise.

Scaling Plan

Alpha Futures' scaling structure is not exhaustively documented in public materials. Based on industry patterns and the firm's three-tier product line, the likely scaling pathway is:

  • Start: Open a Zero, Standard, or Advanced account ($25K–$100K)
  • Qualified Phase: Hit profit targets and maintain consistency to graduate to a funded account with the applicable profit split
  • Scale Opportunity: After demonstrating profitability over 1–3 months, traders may be eligible to request a larger account size (e.g., $50K → $100K, or $100K → $150K+)
  • Scaling Requirements: Likely based on consecutive profitable months, adherence to risk rules, and minimum drawdown ratios. Exact requirements should be clarified with the firm.

No automatic scaling is mentioned.: Unlike some competitors, Alpha Futures does not appear to offer algorithmic scaling. Scaling requests are likely manual and subject to firm approval.

Recommendation: Contact Alpha Futures directly for a detailed scaling roadmap before signing up, especially if your goal is to reach $500K+ accounts within 6 months.

Alpha Futures Rules vs Competitors

Alpha Futures operates in a crowded market. Here's how it compares to industry benchmarks on key rules:

Rule Category Alpha Futures Competitor A (Standard) Competitor B (Aggressive)
Max Drawdown Model EOD Trailing (ressets daily) Static Daily (2–5%) Static Daily (3–6%)
Profit Split (Day 1) 80–90% (plan-dependent) 70–80% 75–85%
Payout Frequency Weekly (after 5 winning days) Monthly Weekly
News Buffer 2 minutes (Standard/Zero) No buffer 5-minute buffer
EA/Bot Prohibition Strict (all plans) Allowed (with approval) Strict (all plans)
Payout Rails 5 (ACH, Wire, SWIFT, Wise, Rise) 2–3 (typically Wire + ACH) 3–4
Payout Processing 48 business hours 3–5 business days 48 business hours

Alpha Futures' Competitive Edges:

  • EOD Trailing Drawdown: More favorable than static daily limits for scalpers; intraday wicks cannot trigger breaches.
  • 90% Split (Advanced/Zero): Higher than most competitors' day-1 splits; only specialized high-performers offer this immediately.
  • Weekly Payouts: Faster than monthly cycles; tied to winning-day milestones, not calendar months.
  • Multi-Rail Payouts: Wise and Rise options reduce conversion costs for international traders.

Alpha Futures' Disadvantages:

  • No Algo Support: A hard blocker for developers; competitors like [Competitor A] allow approved EAs.
  • 2-Minute News Buffer: More restrictive than competitors who allow news trading; traders seeking NFP/FOMC scalps must look elsewhere.
  • Platform Lock: Cannot switch charting platforms mid-account; competitors often allow platform flexibility.
  • UK Entity: May introduce payout friction or regulatory friction for some US traders, though multiple rails mitigate this.

Red Flags and Fine Print

Monthly Subscription During Evaluation: You pay $79–$279/month even before becoming a funded trader. If your evaluation fails, that fee is sunk. Budget for 2–3 months of subscription costs as part of your trading risk.

Platform Lock-In: Once you select a trading platform at signup, you cannot switch without purchasing a new account. If you later prefer a different charting platform, you must restart the evaluation and pay again. This is a significant friction point—choose your platform carefully upfront.

No Free Practice on Live Rules: There is no free tier. You cannot test Alpha Futures' rules, drawdown mechanics, or payout speed without committing a monthly fee. Use a paper account on your broker first to understand EOD trailing drawdown behavior.

EA/Bot Prohibition is Absolute: Even simple automations, trailing-stop algorithms, or order-management systems are prohibited. The firm likely uses trading-behavior analysis to detect automated order patterns. Manual trading only.

Scaling Timeline Unclear: The firm does not publish a public scaling roadmap. You may expect faster scaling than you receive. Ask specific questions during onboarding (e.g., "How long until I'm eligible for a $100K account?") and get responses in writing.

News Buffer Enforcement: The 2-minute buffer is enforced by the firm, not your broker. Attempting to trade during the buffer window may trigger rule violations or account suspension. This is a hard restriction, not a guideline.

Drawdown Calculation Transparency: While EOD trailing drawdown is favorable, the firm does not detail whether drawdown is calculated on account equity, balance, or floating P&L. Request a detailed calculation example before signup.

Withdrawal Minimums and Fees: Not specified in public documentation. Confirm whether there are minimum withdrawal amounts ($100, $250, etc.) and whether the firm charges withdrawal fees (some do; Wise transfers may have small conversion fees).

Profit Target Clarity: The exact profit targets for each evaluation phase are not publicly listed. This is intentionally vague in the trading world (to prevent gaming), but it creates uncertainty. Clarify with support before paying for your first month.

Trustpilot Rating Context: Alpha Futures has a 4.9/5 Trustpilot rating across 2,500+ reviews—exceptionally high in the prop trading space. However, Trustpilot reviews are not verified by trade execution and may reflect customer service satisfaction rather than rule fairness. Read recent 1-star reviews to see common complaints (typically slow scaling, profile rejection, or rule interpretation disputes).

Who Should Trade Alpha Futures?

Ideal candidates: Futures day traders and scalpers who prioritize EOD trailing drawdown rules, traders seeking 90% immediate payouts without consistency requirements (Advanced plan), UK-based traders, and international traders needing Wise or Rise payouts.

Poor fit: Algorithmic traders, Expert Advisor developers, news-event scalpers (due to 2-minute buffer), and traders who need platform flexibility mid-account.

For more details, read the Alpha Futures review page or compare Alpha Futures to other prop firms.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
